content.problemStatement:
Sonya has NNN segments on the xxx-axis. For each valid iii, the iii-th segment contains all points between lil_ili​ and rir_iri​ inclusive.

Maks gave Sonya a magic number KKK. Sonya wants to choose exactly KKK of her segments in such a way that the intersection of the chosen segments is empty, i.e. there is no point which is contained in each of the chosen segments. Find the number of ways in which Sonya can choose KKK segments. Since this number can be large, Sonya only needs to know its remainder modulo 100000000710000000071000000007 (109+710^9+7109+7).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and K. N lines follow, each containing two space-separated integers li and ri.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number of ways to choose the segments, modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: We are looking for pairs of segments. The four pairs with empty intersections are (1,3), (1,5), (2,5) and (4,5).
Explanation: We are looking for pairs of segments. The two pairs with empty intersections are (1,3) and (2,3).
Explanation: We are looking for triples of segments. The two triples with empty intersections are (1,2,4) and (2,3,4).

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use the concept of combinations. We need to choose K segments out of N such that their intersection is empty. We can sort the segments based on their right endpoints and then use a two-pointer technique to find the valid combinations. We will also use modular arithmetic to avoid overflow.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T*N*log(N)) — due to sorting the segments for each test case
